name: Mal Waldron
abstract: Malcolm Earl "Mal" Waldron (August 16, 1925 – December 2, 2002) was an American jazz pianist, composer, and arranger. He started playing professionally in New York in 1950, after graduating from college.

concerts.label: Midnight Performance: Contemporary Jazz Composers Series

about_quotes.speaker: Jackie McLean
about_quotes.evidence: I made two trips to play with Mal Waldron... duo where Mal and I played... play with Mal again, this time with his trio... We had our time together in [Charles] Mingus’s band
about_quotes.doc_title: Jackie McLean — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Chico Freeman
about_quotes.evidence: Mal Waldron, I work with Mal Waldon and Mal Waldron told me once
about_quotes.doc_title: Chico Freeman interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Bert
about_quotes.evidence: I did an album with him at The Bohemia. It was me, George Barrow, Mal Waldron and Willie Jones. It was in ’55.
about_quotes.doc_title: Eddie Bert interviewed by Monk Rowe, Danbury, Connecticut, November 20, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Ron Carter
about_quotes.evidence: Called Where? with Eric Dolphy, Mal Waldron, Charlie Persip, and George Duvivier.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ron Carter — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Abbey Lincoln
about_quotes.evidence: a song I had written with Mal Waldron... Mal Waldron set it to music
about_quotes.doc_title: Abbey Lincoln — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Charles Mingus
about_quotes.evidence: my piano player Mal Waldron gave us when (inaudible) was a cue.
